title: DIY Upholstered Storage Bench
summary: This DIY upholstered storage bench is perfect for adding storage to the end of the bed and also makes a nice place to sit and put on shoes before leaving the house.
description: This DIY upholstered storage bench is perfect for adding storage to the end of the bed and also makes a nice place to sit and put on shoes before leaving the house. The simple design is easy to build from a single plywood sheet. For storage and style, this is the build you're looking for!

steps.title: Cut Down Plywood Sheet
steps.body: Using a circular saw and Kreg Rip Cut (or straight edge), cut a 16" wide plywood strip and two 9" wide plywood strips from your 3/4" plywood sheet. Then, using either a circular saw or miter saw, cut two 16" x 46" pieces to use for the top and bottom panels, two 9" x 44 1/2" pieces to use as the front and back panels and two 9" x 16" pieces to use for the sides. Save the remaining plywood to use for the base later.

steps.title: Assemble Bench Box
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the bottom edges of all the box pieces and along the ends of the front and back box pieces, then assemble box together using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach Bottom
steps.body: Then, screw this box onto the bottom panel using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Upholster Seat
steps.body: Cut 2" foam to fit on seat top and attach using spray adhesive. Cover in batting and staple in place on underside of seat. For more upholstery details, check out the video and tutorial links in the extras tab of this plan.

steps.title: Upholster Bench Box
steps.body: Staple batting onto top edge of bench box and wrap around the sides. Overlap the bottom corner and staple in place on bottom side of bench. Cover 4 sides of plywood box with batting. For more upholstery details, check the links in the extras tab of this plan.

steps.title: Cover with Fabric
steps.body: Repeat steps 4 & 5 with fabric of your choice making sure to completely cover all the batting. For more details on upholstery, check out the links in the extras tab of this plan.

steps.title: Attach Top
steps.body: Attach top of bench to bench box using either a 30" piano hinge OR two small cabinet hinges. If desired, install lid supports or soft close mechanisms.

steps.title: Build Base Frame
steps.body: Cut two strips of plywood to 2 1/2" wide (OR use 1x3s for this), then cut two pieces 46" long and two pieces 11" long. Drill 3/4" pocket holes on the ends of the 11" pieces and assemble a frame like shown using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Apply edge banding to cover the exposed edges if desired.

steps.title: Cut Legs
steps.body: Cut four legs like shown from either the remaining sheet of plywood OR 1x material of your choosing using a jig saw.

steps.title: Attach Legs to Base
steps.body: Attach legs to base frame from step 8 using two 2" wood screws per leg. Install so that each leg is approx. 1 1/2" in from the corner and is angled 45 degrees.

steps.title: Attach Base to Bench
steps.body: Flip bench upside down and install base onto bottom side using 1 1/4" wood screws. Use three on the long side and 1-2 on the short ends.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Finish as desired. For tufted detail, check the links in the extras tab for details.
